prompt: Can you review this code and tell me what's wrong with it? [OrderProcessor with switch statements, long method, SRP violations, hardcoded credentials]

assertions.description: Uses the Refactoring Guru name 'Switch Statements' (or equivalent) to identify the conditional-on-type smell
assertions.check: response mentions 'Switch Statements' or 'conditional' or 'polymorphism' in relation to the item-type if/elif chain

assertions.description: Identifies the Long Method smell by name
assertions.check: response mentions 'Long Method' or 'method is too long' or 'Extract Method'

assertions.description: Identifies that the method is doing too many things (pricing + email + DB) — references SRP or Large Class or divergent responsibilities
assertions.check: response mentions single responsibility, or that the method handles multiple concerns (pricing, email, database)

assertions.description: Recommends at least one named refactoring technique (e.g., Extract Method, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Extract Class)
assertions.check: response mentions at least one specific named technique from Refactoring Guru

assertions.description: Uses Refactoring Guru vocabulary (smell names, technique names) rather than generic descriptions
assertions.check: response uses at least 2 canonical Refactoring Guru terms (smell or technique names)

assertions.description: Mentions Strategy pattern as an option for the discount calculation conditionals
assertions.check: response mentions Strategy pattern in the context of the discount/type-based logic
